title: schradera ternata
intro: Schradera ternata, also known as the three-leafed schradera, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae. It is a shrub native to tropical regions, with various medicinal properties attributed to its leaves and stems. Traditionally, Schradera ternata has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and has been reported to have potential health benefits such as antioxidant and anti-cancer properties.
